papers.name: Reading
papers.coverage_notes: Graded approach; tests reading assessment objectives including comprehension, inference, tone, views/arguments and language features.

papers.name: Writing
papers.coverage_notes: Tests writing assessment objectives including audience/purpose, organisation, coherence, tone/style/register and language use.

papers.name: Listening and Integrated Skills
papers.coverage_notes: Graded approach; tests listening objectives and integrated skills combining listening/reading/writing.

papers.name: Speaking
papers.coverage_notes: Tests speaking assessment objectives including interaction, coherent speech, pronunciation and non-verbal features.

assessment_objectives.name: Reading
assessment_objectives.description: Understand and interpret purpose and meaning; identify main theme and key details; understand contextual meaning; recognise tone/mood; distinguish/evaluate views/arguments; understand language features; interpret/analyse/select/organise ideas from sources.

assessment_objectives.name: Writing
assessment_objectives.description: Write for different contexts, audiences and purposes; convey meaning with vocabulary, devices and patterns; plan and produce coherent structured texts; use appropriate tone, style, register and genre; draft and revise.

assessment_objectives.name: Listening
assessment_objectives.description: Understand and interpret spoken texts; identify key details; understand speakers' feelings, views, attitudes and intentions; recognise a range of accents/varieties; understand language features in spoken texts.

assessment_objectives.name: Speaking
assessment_objectives.description: Express information and ideas with elaboration; convey meaning with vocabulary and patterns; establish and maintain exchanges; produce coherent structured speeches; use intelligible pronunciation; control pace, volume, intonation, stress, eye contact and gesture.

difficulty_ladder.description: single skill recall or direct comprehension (e.g., identify a text type, locate a detail, write a short functional text)
multi-skill application within one paper or strand (e.g., infer tone and evaluate an argument in a reading passage; organise a coherent short essay)
cross-skill synthesis or exam-style integration (e.g., integrate listening data and reading sources into an extended written response)

lesson_use.notes: v1 skill supports classification and qbank routing only. Teacher/student/parent modes are not enabled.

exclude_from_questions: content beyond S4-S6 HKDSE English Language scope
text types not listed in the C&A Guide appendices
live examination regulations not covered by the guide

marking_guidance.notes: Command words and assessment demands are summarised from the C&A Guide p.63. Use generated marking guidance only; do not copy official mark schemes.
